Share Incentive Plan
The SIP Trustees (VG Corporate Trustee (UK) Limited) acquired a total of 7,280 shares (the "Partnership Shares") on 9th June 2026 at a price of £0.596999 per Ordinary Share and a total aggregate cost of £4,346.15. The Partnership Shares were acquired through market purchases and have been allocated to those Company employees participating in its Share Incentive Plan (SIP) scheme.
The SIP scheme is designed to reward and incentivise employees of the Company through tax-efficient salary sacrifice and a free matching award of Ordinary Shares on a one-for-one basis. latest.title: TPXimpact announces £16m contract with the MoJ
latest.content:
1 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 3700G
TPXimpact Holdings PLC
01 June 2026
1 June 2026
TPXimpact Holdings PLC
("TPXimpact", or the "Group", or the "Company")
TPXimpact announces £16 million contract with the Ministry of Justice
TPXimpact Holdings PLC (AIM: TPX), a leading digital transformation partner, is pleased to announce that it has been selected as the successful bidder for a
£16 million, 2-year contract
with the
Ministry of Justice (MoJ)
.
Following a competitive tender process, the Company has been awarded Product Team Bench Model Services. This award builds upon TPXimpact's existing work within the Ministry of Justice, supporting His Majesty's Prison and Probation Service to deliver digital transformation across Probation Services.
Modernising legal aid and wider justice digital services
TPXimpact's contract will see a range of multidisciplinary product teams deployed to support transformation programmes in the Legal Aid Agency (LAA) and other service transformation programmes across the wider MoJ estate.
Our people will work alongside LAA and Justice Digital teams to drive modernisation across the estate including the replacement of complex legacy systems and delivery of advanced legal aid experiences to benefit users of the agency's services such as citizens, case workers and legal aid providers. We will help to build resilient, adaptable tools and systems that support operational changes while protecting sensitive public data.
The work will support Justice Digital and the LAA to continue delivering on their strategic priorities for 2026-2029 as part of a wider £350m investment in building a world-class justice system that addresses system pressures and improves outcomes for all justice users.
New business progressing well
This £16 million award brings total new business for the first two months of FY27 to £31 million. This follows a series of new win announcements and contract uplifts in the last quarter of FY26 - DEFRA (£39 million), NHS England (£22 million) and HMLR (£11 million).
Björn Conway, Chief Executive Officer of TPXimpact, commented:
"This award from the Ministry of Justice will enable us to help on a number of service transformations in the Legal Aid Agency and across their other agencies. We are thrilled to be working with the Ministry of Justice and appreciate the trust they have placed in us to support their ongoing transformation efforts. Our teams are certainly up for the challenge! After a strong Q4, our £31m of new contract wins in the first 2 months of this year underpins TPXimpact's strong growth trajectory."
Notice of Results
The Group will announce preliminary unaudited Full Year Results for FY26 and its initial outlook for FY27 on Tuesday, 16th June 2026.
Analyst Presentation
Björn Conway, CEO, and Noel Douglas, CFO, will host a virtual analyst presentation at 9:30am on the day. Any analyst wishing to attend is invited to register by emailing tpx@almastrategic.com.
Investor Presentation
The management team will also be hosting a virtual investor presentation at 1:00pm via the Investor Meet Company platform.
The presentation is open to all existing and potential shareholders. Questions can be submitted pre-event via the Investor Meet Company dashboard up until 9:00am the day before the meeting or at any time during the live presentation.
Investors can sign up to Investor Meet Company for free via:
https://www.investormeetcompany.com/tpximpact-holdings-plc/register-investor

latest.title: Full Year Trading Update and Notice of Results
latest.content:
22 Apr 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 3813B
TPXimpact Holdings PLC
22 April 2026
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR"), and is disclosed in accordance with the company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR.
22 April 2026
TPXimpact Holdings PLC
("TPXimpact", or the "Group", or the "Company")
Full Year Trading Update and Notice of Results
Trading significantly ahead of the recently upgraded guidance
TPXimpact Holdings PLC (AIM: TPX), a leading technology-enabled services company focused on people-powered digital transformation, provides a trading update for the financial year ended 31 March 2026 ("FY26").
Following a strong final quarter, the Group is pleased to report that it expects to comfortably exceed the recently upgraded consensus* across all key financial metrics.
Subject to completion of the year-end audit, the Group is expected to report:
● Revenue of c.£78.1m (FY25: £77.3m), representing 1% year on year growth and finishing ahead of market consensus of £76.2m*;
● Gross Margin improvement to 31.7% (FY25: 28.6%), reflecting a 310 basis point uplift driven by improved project mix and operational efficiencies;
● Adjusted EBITDA** of c.£8.6m (FY25: £5.6m) a 54% year on year increase;
● Adjusted EBITDA** margin increased to 11.0% (FY25: 7.3%), as a result of the improvement in gross margin and tight control of discretionary costs and other overheads;
● Significantly improved net debt position with net debt as at 31 March 2026 of c.£4.2m (FY25: £8.5m), a 50% year on year reduction, reflecting strong cash generation of the Company in the year;
● Leverage (Net Debt/Adjusted EBITDA**) of 0.5x (FY25: 1.5x), reflecting a significant year on year improvement.
Strategic position: Turnaround complete
The Group's performance in FY26 marks the successful conclusion of its three-year strategic turnaround. Having met or exceeded all key financial and operational targets, the Group has established a profitable, cash-generative platform with a significantly healthier balance sheet.
TPXimpact is now entering its new three-year strategic phase focused on sustainable growth, supported by new business wins of £122m and the recent appointment of Emma Broom as Chief Growth Officer. The Company looks forward to unveiling this new comprehensive three-year plan that will define our next chapter of growth in the next few months.
Bjorn Conway, Chief Executive Officer, commented:
"I am delighted by the performance of the business during the last financial year, which provides a positive conclusion to our three-year turnaround plan. We have successfully reshaped the business into a more profitable, resilient and cash-generative organisation. With adjusted EBITDA margin now at 11.0% and leverage down to 0.5x, we have a very stable base on which to grow as we move into FY27."
Notice of Results:
The Group expects to announce its preliminary unaudited Full Year Results for FY26 and initial outlook for FY27 on 16th June 2026.
Notes:
*Based on the latest published equity research, the company understands current market consensus for the year ended 31 March 2026 (FY26), as at 21 April 2026, being the day prior to the publication of this announcement, to be revenue of £76.2m, gross margin of 30.5%, Adjusted EBITDA of £7.1m, Net Debt of £5.7m, and Leverage of 0.8x.
**Adjusted EBITDA comprises earnings before interest, tax, depreciation and amortisation further adjusted for exceptional items and share based payments.

company_30s.what_they_do: TPX delivers secure, cloud‑based digital services to the UK government and public sector. Think of it as a tech partner that builds, hosts and manages bespoke software platforms for ministries, NHS trusts and local councils.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The public‑sector tech market is worth billions and is locked in a slow‑moving transformation. TPX’s contracts give it a foothold in a highly regulated, repeat‑business environment where data security and compliance are non‑negotiable.
company_30s.current_state: TPX has recently signed a £16 m MoJ contract and secured a £22 m NHS deal, signalling a steady stream of revenue. The company is still in a growth phase, investing in new contracts and upgrading its platform, but its share price sits near a historic low that suggests a deep sell‑off has taken place.

rns_narrative: TPX’s latest releases show a pattern of contract wins and incremental revenue growth. The MoJ and NHS contracts are the biggest stories, followed by a series of financial updates that keep the company’s earnings outlook on a positive trajectory.
Governance moves – a new non‑executive director and an auditor change – signal a fresh start and a push for greater transparency.
The share‑incentive plan and the Q3 trading update suggest the company is preparing for a more aggressive expansion phase while keeping an eye on cash burn.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – the chatter is sparse but bullish, focused on the new government deals.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish voices highlight the 80p target set by Cavendish and the recent contract upticks, framing TPX as a winner in the public‑sector tech race.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bearish chatter is limited but points to the company’s thin profit margins and the risk that government contracts can be volatile. Concerns also surface around the timing of the next earnings release.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The community is largely silent, with a few bullish posts surfacing after the MoJ announcement. Sentiment is poised to shift upward once the June results are in.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is dead – there’s no pre‑existing hype, so a well‑timed catalyst could create a big surprise.

near_term_catalysts: June 16 earnings – the company will report on the MoJ contract performance and any new deals.
Potential new public‑sector tenders announced in the next quarter.
Any sign of a price break above the 60p support level on a surge of volume.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike that pushes the price above 60p, a clear break of the 58p support, and a positive earnings surprise. Also monitor the share‑incentive plan for any dilution that could weigh on the price.
expect_next.timing_regime: TPX appears to be in the late‑stage washout of a deep crash. The APEX score suggests a tight price band – a small move could trigger a rebound if the market starts to re‑price the contract pipeline.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: TPX is sitting on a deep sell‑off and has just secured sizeable government contracts that provide a clear revenue runway. Historically, deep‑crash signals (70‑95% down) have produced 2‑to‑3× returns once a catalyst appears. If the stock recovers to 80p from its current 55p level, that’s a 45% upside – a classic asymmetric play.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest risk is that the company’s margins remain thin and it may miss the June earnings target. If the MoJ contract underperforms or a new tender falls through, the price could retreat back to the 50p range or lower.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Risk: 10% downside to 45p. Reward: 45% upside to 80p. The reward‑to‑risk ratio is roughly 4.5:1, a very favourable asymmetry for a contrarian position.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a volume‑driven break above 60p, size the position to risk no more than 1.5% of the portfolio per trade, and set a stop 5p below the 58p support. Use a trailing stop at 5p below the high once the price moves above 70p.
playbook.if_watching: Hold a small position or stay out until the June earnings release. A clear earnings beat or a new contract announcement would be the trigger to step in.
playbook.if_passing: If the company announces a material downgrade, a delisting notice, or a significant fraud claim, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: TPX is a deep‑crashed public‑sector tech play that has just landed big government contracts – a perfect contrarian setup with a 4‑to‑5× upside potential if the June earnings surprise the market.
